methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Last Energy, a Series C nuclear energy company founded by Bret Kugelmass, has raised $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private nuclear energy firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised of $100M as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement yields an implied valuation of $170M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow nuclear energy set; while 8 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 fall within relevant valuation tiers, making a peer-based estimate unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $144M to $195M, with a median point of $170M. This range reflects the methodological constraint of relying on a single applicable triangulation method rather than cross-verification across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for this capital stage within frontier nuclear infrastructure, the true comparability of public nuclear energy equities to private frontier-stage development, and the absence of direct transaction data for recent Series C rounds in the sector. A fuller triangulation would require either additional same-stage nuclear comparables or validation through discounted cash flow analysis tied to project timelines.
